objective: Test Nexus under a difficult real research-and-engineering workload by determining whether the cost of creating and maintaining many independent, aged, credible online identities is linear, superlinear or regime-dependent, and by producing a falsifiable model plus a small inspectable artifact.
research_question: Under realistic adversarial conditions, how does the marginal cost of fabricating and maintaining N mutually independent, aged and credible online identities scale with N, and which mechanisms or empirical observations distinguish linear, superlinear and regime-dependent cost models?

continue_without_interruption_unless: a consequential product or research preference cannot be inferred safely
new evidence contradicts the declared task boundary
credentials, local-only files or machine-specific execution become necessary

constraints: Do not modify baseline-001, canonical snapshots, historical audit targets, audit ledgers or preserved evidence.
Do not treat model agreement as external validation.
Separate sourced evidence, implementation, inference, hypothesis, falsification and user-authorized disposition.
Do not collect, purchase, operate or facilitate real fraudulent identities, accounts, credentials or evasion activity.
Use public literature, lawful datasets, defensive research and synthetic modelling only.
Do not promote any output into main without explicit user approval.
A passing test proves only the property exercised.
Minimize user turns and avoid requiring a local PC unless a material task dependency emerges.
required_outputs: evidence register with source provenance and confidence labels
competing linear, superlinear and regime-dependent mechanism models
small inspectable executable or computational artifact
cross-provider or independent-review handoff where available
reconstructable receipt recording context loss, coordination friction and unresolved questions
success_criteria: The work produces a useful external research result independent of Nexus.
Important claims are traceable to evidence or explicitly labelled as inference or hypothesis.
The proposal can be reconstructed from repository artifacts without relying on chat memory.
The user is asked only for consequential preferences or canonical promotion.
The workflow reveals whether Nexus reduces context loss and coordination ambiguity during hard work.
non_claims: One completed vertical slice does not establish general Nexus effectiveness.
A computational model does not establish the real-world scaling law without empirical support.
Public evidence may be incomplete or selection-biased.
This task does not validate live GitHub bootstrap behaviour, complete privacy, complete security or scientific validity.
